subject: Computer Networking
title: Wi-Fi Changes Everything
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Wi-Fi Changes Everything.

hook.text: In 1999, Apple put a wireless card in the iBook. Steve Jobs browsed the web while walking across a stage.

buildup.text: Wi-Fi used the 802.11 standard to send data over radio waves. No cables, no drilling, instant connectivity.

discovery.text: Coffee shops, airports, and homes all added Wi-Fi. By 2005, wireless internet was expected everywhere.

twist.text: Early Wi-Fi was slow and insecure. WEP encryption was cracked within years of its release.

climax.text: Wi-Fi 6 now delivers gigabit speeds wirelessly. The standard keeps evolving to handle more devices and more data.

punchline.text: Wi-Fi untethered the internet. The wire became optional.
